_comment: Copy to config.json and fill every {{PLACEHOLDER}}. The onboarding skill fills this interactively. config.json is gitignored — your details never get committed. Every field here exists because getting it wrong caused a real problem in a live BDR system; the comments explain why each matters.

bdr.name: {{YOUR_FULL_NAME}}
bdr.work_browser_profile: {{which Chrome/browser profile is WORK — the agent must never use a personal profile for work}}

product_glossary._comment: terms your agent should know so it never mis-describes the product

icp._comment: Persona precision matters more than anything. Enrichment tools mis-classify seniority; the TITLE TEXT rules.

scope._comment: The bounded pool the agent may prospect. Off-list = stop.
scope.accounts_shared_across_reps: {{true|false — if true, the agent must verify ownership before touching an account}}
scope.ownership_check: {{how to confirm an account/contact is YOURS and not another rep's, e.g. CRM Account Owner field}}

voice.reference_published_thought_leadership_i: {{true|false — may the agent open by referencing a prospect's PUBLISHED post/blog/talk? Distinct from profile-mining. Default false.}}

cta_rules._comment: CTA style differs by motion. Getting this wrong tanks reply rates.
cta_rules.cold_outbound: {{engagement question | meeting ask — e.g. engagement question that invites them to share their situation}}

sender_rules_by_sequence._comment: if different motions send from different addresses

sequence_ids._comment: YOUR tool's IDs. Never commit real IDs to a shared repo — they live here in gitignored config only.

agent_may_act_in: {{tools the agent may take actions in}}
agent_read_only: {{tools the agent may only read, never post/act}}

research._comment: Non-negotiables that keep quality high and prevent fabrication.

guardrails.approval_model: {{per-send | batch — e.g. one APPROVE SEND per batch, then execute autonomously}}
